reasoning: The informal statement asks to show that the lower limit topology Rl and K-topology on ℝ are not comparable. Two topologies are "comparable" iff one is finer than the other, so "not comparable" means neither topology refines the other — i.e., there exists an Rl-open set that is not K-open AND there exists a K-open set that is not Rl-open. The Lean conjunction `¬(∀U, Rl.IsOpen U → K_topology.IsOpen U) ∧ ¬(∀U, K_topology.IsOpen U → Rl.IsOpen U)` encodes exactly this. The basis definitions match Munkres precisely: Rl uses half-open intervals Ico a b = [a,b); K = {1/(n+1) : n ∈ ℕ} = {1, 1/2, 1/3, ...}; K_topology is generated by open intervals (a,b) together with sets (a,b)\K. All types, quantifiers, and the comparison direction line up correctly.
